title: branch — authorization policies
description: One policy. Reads are broad — every tenant member sees the branches they participate in. Writes are admin+owner only. Close (and reopen) are owner-tier. Cross-tenant reads live on the platform surface.

abilities.scope: resolved via ScopeSuffixResolver. `.tenant` = every branch under the caller's active tenant. `.own` = branches the caller participates in via TenantMember / TeamMember / AthleteEnrollment / Coach.branch_id / Staff.branch_id.
single-row check. Any tenant member can read a branch they have visibility on. Coaches / reception / athletes with `.own` scope only see branches they're linked to.
admin or owner in the active tenant. Also gated by the `branch_slot` entitlement — the observer decrements on create.

admin. Body limited to non-address, non-capacity, non-hours fields when the caller only carries `branches.update`; wider fields require the specific permissions (update_address / update_capacity / update_hours).
admin OR reception (with .branch scope narrowing to the reception's assigned branches). Reception role is typical for a chain gym where the head-of-branch runs the schedule board.
admin. Compliance-audited — every address change writes an audit row.
admin. Manual pin-drop from the map view.
admin. Cannot pause a Branch that's already paused (BRANCH_ALREADY_PAUSED, 409).

owner-tier. Refused when active downstream rows reference the branch (BRANCH_IN_USE, 409). Refused when the branch is the last non-closed branch on its (tenant, organization) AND that organization is not archived (leaving an organization with no active branches is a hard error — the org must be archived first).
owner-tier. Refused when a successor branch was created under the same (tenant, organization) post-close (BRANCH_SUCCESSOR_EXISTS, 409).
admin. Auto-demotes the previous default via the observer.
admin. Also requires `branch_geocoding` entitlement (returns 402 with upgrade path when absent).
ALWAYS DENIES on the tenant surface. Response redirects the caller to `POST /branches/{branch}/close`. Hard-delete lives on the platform-support CLI.
platform_admin only. Cross-tenant read for support tooling.
super_admin. Bypasses the dependency-check kill switch. Every use fires an alert; audit-severity=warn.
